ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN NUTRITIONIST / MICHIGAN

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Michigan Dietitian Nutritionist License

Michigan Dietitian Nutritionist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian Nutritionist applications in Michigan run through Board of Dietetics and Nutrition. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • CDR Registration Card - Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD/RDN).
  • LARA Application (Pending) - Application form will be available through the LARA online licensing portal.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Reciprocity provisions will be defined by the newly formed Board. Active CDR registration will be the primary qualifying credential, making the transition straightforward for out-of-state licensees.

  1. STEP 1

    Create a MiPLUS Account

    Visit the Michigan Professional Licensing User System (MiPLUS) portal at aca-prod.accela.com/LARA/Default.aspx and click 'Register for an Account' to establish your user profile.

    Fee: $130 (Estimated) · Timeline: TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)

  2. STEP 2

    Initiate Online Application

    Log in to your newly created MiPLUS account, select 'Apply for a License,' and choose 'Dietitian' from the list of available license types to begin the application process.

    Fee: $130 (Estimated) · Timeline: TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)

  3. STEP 3

    Complete Application and Upload Documents

    Fill out all required sections of the online application within MiPLUS. This includes providing personal details, educational history, proof of supervised experience, and verification of your Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) certification. Ensure official transcripts are sent directly from your institution if required.

    Fee: $130 (Estimated) · Timeline: TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)

  4. STEP 4

    Pay Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable application fee of $75 as prompted within the MiPLUS system at the time of application submission.

    Fee: $130 (Estimated) · Timeline: TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)

  5. STEP 5

    Monitor Application Status

    Regularly log in to your MiPLUS account to track the status of your application. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Bureau of Professional Licensing (BPL) to avoid delays.

    Fee: $130 (Estimated) · Timeline: TBD (Applications Expected ~2027)

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$75
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$130 (Estimated)

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

517-241-0199

Application page

Renewal

Dietitian Nutritionist renewal in Michigan follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annual (Estimated based on fee schedule)
Renewal fee
$55
Continuing education
45 hours every 3 years

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Michigan currently accepting dietitian license applications?

Not yet. Although Public Act 39 of 2024 reinstated licensure and the Board approved administrative rules in October 2025, applications are expected to open approximately 18 months after the rules are formally promulgated.

How much will a Michigan dietitian license cost?

The established fees are a $75 application processing fee and a $55 annual license fee, for a total initial cost of $130.

Can I practice in Michigan without a state license right now?

Yes. Until the application process opens and the law is fully enforced, practicing with a CDR credential in Michigan remains the standard. Most employers require the RD/RDN credential.

Does Michigan have reciprocity for dietitian nutritionists?

Michigan is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian nutritionist license to Michigan?

This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?

There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$130 (Estimated)

Processing time

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · $55

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annual (Estimated based on fee schedule) · $55

Licensing board
